claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 16 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 16 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 16 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 16 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, could you please tell me how many children you would like to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Thanks for your help. I'd like to claim Child Benefit for my child Alex, who is 16 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Since Alex is 16 years old, I need to ask a few more questions to determine your eligibility.

Is Alex currently in approved education (such as A-levels, Scottish Highers, or NVQs up to level 3) or approved training (an unpaid course that isn't provided through a contract of employment)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any approved education at the moment. They're not doing A-levels or anything like that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since Alex is 16 and not currently in approved education or training, I have a couple more questions:

Has Alex recently left education or training (within the last 20 weeks), and if so, have they registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex hasn't registered with any careers service or the armed forces. They just left school and haven't signed up for anything like that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. I have a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

1. Is Alex currently working 24 hours or more per week?
2. Is Alex receiving any benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't work 24 hours a week or anything like that. And they're not receiving any benefits in their own name either.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I just have a few more questions about your circumstances:

1. Do you live in the UK?
2. Does Alex live with you, or do you contribute at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit amount towards their upkeep (for example, on food, clothes, or pocket money)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm here in England. And yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. I have one more question:

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Unfortunately, based on what you've told me, you would not be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex. Here's why:

- Alex is 16 years old
- Alex is not in approved education or training
- Alex has not registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services within 20 weeks of leaving education

Under the Child Benefit rules, you can normally only continue to receive Child Benefit for a child who is 16 or over if they remain in approved education or training. There is an exception that allows you to apply for Child Benefit to continue for 20 weeks if a 16 or 17 year old leaves education and registers with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services, but Alex hasn't done this.

Let me record this outcome for you.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on my assessment, **you are not currently eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

**Summary:**
- You meet the residency requirement (living in the UK) and the responsibility requirement (Alex lives with you)
- However, since Alex is 16 and is not in approved education or training, you do not qualify for Child Benefit

**What could change this?**
If Alex were to:
1. **Enrol in approved education or training** (such as A-levels, NVQs up to level 3, or an approved unpaid training course), you could then claim Child Benefit until Alex turns 20 (as long as they remain in approved education/training), OR
2. **Register with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services**, you could apply for Child Benefit to continue for up to 20 weeks

I hope this information is helpful. If Alex's circumstances change, you may wish to revisit your eligibility at that time. Is there anything else I can help you with?

reasonings: Alex is 16 years old and is no longer in approved education or training. Under Child Benefit rules, you normally only qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 16, OR if your child is under 20 and stays in approved education or training. Since Alex is 16 and not in approved education or training, this condition is not met. Additionally, there is an exception that allows Child Benefit to continue for 20 weeks if a 16 or 17 year old leaves education and registers with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services - however, Alex has not registered with either of these. Alex is not working 24+ hours per week and is not receiving benefits in their own right, but these factors alone do not confer eligibility - the child must still meet the age/education requirements. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK and Alex lives with them, so the residency and responsibility criteria are met. However, since Alex is 16 and not in approved education or training, and has not registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services, the claimant does not qualify for Child Benefit for Alex. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

However, since Alex is 16 and not in approved education or training, and has not registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services, the claimant does not qualify for Child Benefit for Alex. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.
